query_normalized: investigating the impact of code ownership on llm code understanding computer science
rationale: The paper focuses on the architecture and training of a model for code-text generation (Java), which is a distinct task from investigating the impact of "code ownership" (a social/organizational construct) on LLM understanding. It does not measure code ownership, study the specific mechanism of ownership effects, or provide a baseline for that variable, failing to meet any of the inclusion criteria (a-f) while sharing only the broad domain of "code and LLMs."
